title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: How novel is that research paper? Competition to quantify concept crowns winner
items.description: The automated tool could be used to help journals vet submissions

items.title: Protein name confusion created antibody mix-up affecting hundreds of papers
items.description: Cancer and cell aging studies may have relied on antibodies to incorrect molecule

items.title: Satellite maps of sinking coastlines come under scrutiny
items.description: Radar studies produce contradictory results, complicating efforts to gauge sea level rise in vulnerable areas

items.title: Extinct brown bear had a surprising diet
items.description: Unlike their modern counterparts, Atlas bears were strict vegetarians

items.title: Does a distant alien world harbor promising signs of life? Most astrobiologists say no
items.description: Poll of nearly 500 scientists reveals how rapid surveys can take researchers’ pulse on controversial topics

items.title: AI executives join call for stricter regulation of synthetic biology
items.description: Letter urges Congress to require DNA firms to screen orders to prevent AI-aided bioweapons

items.title: House spending panel proposes slight raise for NIH in 2027
items.description: Draft bill rejects Trump’s plan to slash and rejigger the $47 billion biomedical research agency

items.title: Amid a flood of AI advances, astrophysicists are questioning the soul of their field
items.description: Researchers see enormous power in new tools—but also the potential end of astrophysics as a human endeavor

items.title: Bees just did something no other insect has been shown to do
items.description: Like humans, bumble bees can plan ahead when working through a problem, even pausing to consider the solution

items.title: U.S. health agencies join fight against ‘ultraprocessed foods’—whatever they are
items.description: Some scientists criticize label as ambiguous and confusing; others call it a valuable lens on diet
